Orchestration¶

The orchestration module provides compute orchestration for validation jobs. It implements smart job splitting to avoid Docker-in-Docker issues and enables parallel execution of WASM and container checks.

Note

This module was moved from griot-registry to griot-core to provide better separation of concerns. The registry now imports orchestration from griot_core.orchestration.

Overview¶

The key challenge in distributed validation is handling mixed check types:

  • WASM checks: Run inside a worker using embedded WASM runtime

  • Container checks: Require running a container

Running containers inside containers (Docker-in-Docker) is problematic in Kubernetes environments. The orchestration module solves this by:

  1. Splitting jobs by runtime type (JobSplitter)

  2. Running WASM checks in a single worker container

  3. Spawning container checks as native K8s pods

  4. Running all jobs in parallel and aggregating results (ResultAggregator)

Architecture¶

Contract with mixed checks
        │
        ▼
┌──────────────────┐
│   JobSplitter    │  Separates WASM vs Container checks
└──────────────────┘
        │
        ├──► WASM checks ────► griot-core worker pod (runs all WASM)
        │                              │
        ├──► Container check A ──► K8s pod (native)  │
        │                                            ├──► PARALLEL
        ├──► Container check B ──► K8s pod (native)  │
        │                                            │
        ▼                                            │
┌──────────────────┐                                 │
│ ResultAggregator │ ◄───────────────────────────────┘
└──────────────────┘
        │
        ▼
  AggregatedResult

Key Benefits¶

  1. No Docker-in-Docker: Container checks run as native K8s pods

  2. Parallel execution: WASM and container checks run simultaneously

  3. Efficient WASM batching: All WASM checks run in one container

  4. Result aggregation: Combines results from all parallel jobs

Quick Start¶

from griot_core.orchestration import (
    ValidationOrchestrator,
    DispatcherConfig,
    ComputeBackend,
)

# Configure for Kubernetes
config = DispatcherConfig(
    backend=ComputeBackend.KUBERNETES,
    wasm_worker_image="griot/wasm-worker:v1.0",
    timeout_seconds=600,
)

# Create orchestrator
orchestrator = ValidationOrchestrator(
    dispatcher_config=config,
    callback_base_url="https://registry.example.com",
    namespace="griot",
)

# Execute validation
result = await orchestrator.validate(
    contract=my_contract,
    profile="data_engineering",
    data_reference={"s3": "s3://bucket/data.parquet"},
)

print(f"Valid: {result.is_valid}")
print(f"Passed: {result.passed_checks}/{result.total_checks}")

Components¶

ValidationOrchestrator¶

The main entry point for orchestrated validation.

class ValidationOrchestrator:
    def __init__(
        self,
        dispatcher_config: DispatcherConfig | None = None,
        dispatcher: ComputeDispatcher | None = None,
        splitter: JobSplitter | None = None,
        callback_base_url: str | None = None,
        **dispatcher_kwargs,
    ): ...

Methods:

  • validate(contract, profile, data_reference, ...): Execute validation

  • validate_checks(job_id, checks, ...): Lower-level API with explicit checks

  • receive_callback(job_id, result): Handle worker callback

  • get_aggregator(job_id): Get result aggregator for a job

JobSplitter¶

Separates checks by runtime type.

from griot_core.orchestration import JobSplitter, CheckSpec, CheckRuntime

splitter = JobSplitter()

checks = [
    CheckSpec(
        name="null_check",
        executor_uri="registry://null-check@1.0",
        runtime=CheckRuntime.WASM,
    ),
    CheckSpec(
        name="drift_check",
        executor_uri="oci://ghcr.io/griot/drift:1.0",
        runtime=CheckRuntime.CONTAINER,
    ),
]

split_job = splitter.split(
    job_id="job-123",
    contract_id="orders",
    contract_version="1.0.0",
    profile="data_engineering",
    checks=checks,
    data_reference={"s3": "s3://bucket/data.parquet"},
)

# split_job.wasm_job contains WASM checks
# split_job.container_jobs contains one job per container check

ResultAggregator¶

Collects results from parallel executions.

from griot_core.orchestration import ResultAggregator

aggregator = ResultAggregator(split_job)
aggregator.start()

# As results come in from callbacks
aggregator.add_wasm_result(wasm_callback_data)
aggregator.add_container_result(container_callback_data)

# Check completion
if aggregator.is_complete:
    result = aggregator.aggregate()
    print(f"Valid: {result.is_valid}")

Compute Dispatchers¶

ComputeDispatcher Interface¶

All dispatchers implement this interface:

class ComputeDispatcher(ABC):
    @abstractmethod
    async def dispatch_wasm_worker(self, spec: WasmJobSpec) -> DispatchResult:
        """Dispatch WASM checks to a worker container."""
        ...

    @abstractmethod
    async def dispatch_container(self, spec: ContainerJobSpec) -> DispatchResult:
        """Dispatch a single container check as a native pod."""
        ...

    @abstractmethod
    async def check_status(self, invocation_id: str) -> dict:
        """Check job status."""
        ...

    @abstractmethod
    async def cancel(self, invocation_id: str) -> bool:
        """Cancel a running job."""
        ...

KubernetesDispatcher¶

Creates native K8s Jobs for both WASM workers and container checks.

from griot_core.orchestration import (
    KubernetesDispatcher,
    DispatcherConfig,
    ComputeBackend,
)

config = DispatcherConfig(
    backend=ComputeBackend.KUBERNETES,
    wasm_worker_image="griot/wasm-worker:v1.0",
    timeout_seconds=600,
    memory_mb=1024,
    cpu_millicores=1000,
    labels={"app": "griot"},
)

dispatcher = KubernetesDispatcher(
    config,
    namespace="griot",
    service_account="griot-worker",
)

LambdaDispatcher¶

Uses AWS Lambda for serverless execution.

from griot_core.orchestration import LambdaDispatcher

dispatcher = LambdaDispatcher(
    config,
    wasm_function_name="griot-wasm-worker",
    container_function_prefix="griot-check-",
)

CloudRunDispatcher¶

Uses Google Cloud Run for serverless containers.

from griot_core.orchestration import CloudRunDispatcher

dispatcher = CloudRunDispatcher(
    config,
    project_id="my-project",
    region="us-central1",
    wasm_service_url="https://griot-wasm-worker-xxx.run.app",
)

LocalDispatcher¶

For development and testing.

from griot_core.orchestration import LocalDispatcher

dispatcher = LocalDispatcher(config, max_workers=4)

Factory Function¶

from griot_core.orchestration import create_dispatcher, DispatcherConfig

config = DispatcherConfig(backend=ComputeBackend.KUBERNETES, ...)
dispatcher = create_dispatcher(config, namespace="griot")

Type Definitions¶

CheckSpec¶

@dataclass
class CheckSpec:
    name: str
    executor_uri: str
    runtime: CheckRuntime  # WASM or CONTAINER
    parameters: dict[str, Any] = field(default_factory=dict)
    severity: str = "warning"
    timeout_seconds: int = 300

    @classmethod
    def from_executor_uri(cls, name: str, executor_uri: str, **kwargs):
        """Create CheckSpec, inferring runtime from URI."""

WasmJobSpec¶

@dataclass
class WasmJobSpec:
    job_id: str
    contract_id: str
    contract_version: str
    profile: str
    checks: list[CheckSpec]  # All WASM checks batched together
    data_reference: dict[str, Any]
    callback_url: str | None = None
    timeout_seconds: int = 600

ContainerJobSpec¶

@dataclass
class ContainerJobSpec:
    job_id: str
    parent_job_id: str
    contract_id: str
    contract_version: str
    check: CheckSpec  # Single container check
    data_reference: dict[str, Any]
    image: str | None = None  # Extracted from executor_uri
    timeout_seconds: int = 600
    resource_limits: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)

SplitJob¶

@dataclass
class SplitJob:
    parent_job_id: str
    wasm_job: WasmJobSpec | None  # If any WASM checks
    container_jobs: list[ContainerJobSpec]  # One per container check
    total_checks: int

    @property
    def has_wasm_checks(self) -> bool: ...
    @property
    def has_container_checks(self) -> bool: ...

AggregatedResult¶

@dataclass
class AggregatedResult:
    job_id: str
    contract_id: str
    contract_version: str
    profile: str
    is_valid: bool
    total_checks: int
    passed_checks: int
    failed_checks: int
    check_results: list[CheckResultItem]
    wasm_execution_time_ms: float | None
    container_execution_time_ms: float | None
    total_execution_time_ms: float | None
    errors: list[str]

DispatcherConfig¶

@dataclass
class DispatcherConfig:
    backend: ComputeBackend
    wasm_worker_image: str = "griot/wasm-worker:latest"
    timeout_seconds: int = 600
    memory_mb: int = 512
    cpu_millicores: int = 1000
    retry_count: int = 3
    environment: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)
    labels: dict[str, str] = field(default_factory=dict)

WASM Worker¶

The WasmWorker class runs inside the WASM worker container.

from griot_core.workers import WasmWorker

# Entry point for container
async def main():
    worker = WasmWorker()
    result = await worker.run()  # Reads spec from env vars

# Or execute specific checks
worker = WasmWorker()
result = await worker.execute_wasm_checks(wasm_job_spec)

Environment Variables:

  • GRIOT_JOB_SPEC: JSON-serialized WasmJobSpec

  • GRIOT_JOB_ID: Job identifier

  • GRIOT_CALLBACK_URL: URL to POST results

Integration with Registry¶

The griot-registry uses the orchestration module:

# In griot-registry/services/validation_jobs.py
from griot_core.orchestration import (
    ValidationOrchestrator,
    ComputeBackend,
    DispatcherConfig,
)

class ValidationJobService:
    def __init__(self, storage, settings):
        self._orchestrator = None

    @property
    def orchestrator(self) -> ValidationOrchestrator:
        if self._orchestrator is None:
            config = DispatcherConfig(
                backend=ComputeBackend(self.settings.compute_backend),
                wasm_worker_image=self.settings.wasm_worker_image,
            )
            self._orchestrator = ValidationOrchestrator(
                dispatcher_config=config,
                callback_base_url=self.settings.callback_base_url,
            )
        return self._orchestrator

API Reference¶

Griot Core Orchestration Module.

This module provides compute orchestration for validation jobs, including smart job splitting to avoid Docker-in-Docker issues.

Architecture:
Contract with mixed checks

v

┌──────────────────┐ │ JobSplitter │ Separates WASM vs Container checks └──────────────────┘


├──► WASM checks ────► griot-core worker pod (runs all WASM) | | ├──► Container check A ──► K8s pod (native) | | ├──► PARALLEL ├──► Container check B ──► K8s pod (native) | | | v |

┌──────────────────┐ | │ ResultAggregator │ ◄───────────────────────────────┘ └──────────────────┘


v

AggregatedResult

Key components: - ValidationOrchestrator: Main entry point for orchestrated validation - JobSplitter: Separates WASM and container checks - ResultAggregator: Combines results from parallel executions - ComputeDispatcher: Interface for compute backends (K8s, Lambda, etc.)

Example

from griot_core.orchestration import (

ValidationOrchestrator, DispatcherConfig, ComputeBackend,

)

# Configure for Kubernetes config = DispatcherConfig(

backend=ComputeBackend.KUBERNETES, wasm_worker_image=”griot/wasm-worker:v1.0”,

)

orchestrator = ValidationOrchestrator(

dispatcher_config=config, namespace=”griot”,

)

# Execute validation result = await orchestrator.validate(

contract=my_contract, profile=”data_engineering”, data_reference={“s3”: “s3://bucket/data.parquet”},

)

class griot_core.orchestration.AggregatedResult(job_id, contract_id, contract_version, profile, is_valid, total_checks, passed_checks, failed_checks, check_results, wasm_execution_time_ms=None, container_execution_time_ms=None, total_execution_time_ms=None, started_at=None, completed_at=None, errors=<factory>, metadata=<factory>)[source]¶

Aggregated result from all parallel job executions.

Combines results from the WASM worker and individual container checks into a single validation result.

class griot_core.orchestration.JobSplitter(default_wasm_timeout=600, default_container_timeout=600)[source]¶

Bases: object

Splits validation jobs into WASM and container sub-jobs.

The splitter categorizes checks by their runtime type and creates appropriate job specifications for each:

  • WASM checks are batched into a single WasmJobSpec that will run in a griot-core worker container using the embedded WASM runtime.

  • Container checks each become a separate ContainerJobSpec that will run as a native K8s pod (avoiding Docker-in-Docker).

Example

splitter = JobSplitter()

# Contract has mixed checks checks = [

CheckSpec(name=”null_check”, executor_uri=”registry://null-check@1.0”, runtime=CheckRuntime.WASM), CheckSpec(name=”drift_check”, executor_uri=”oci://ghcr.io/griot/drift:1.0”, runtime=CheckRuntime.CONTAINER),

]

split = splitter.split(

job_id=”job-123”, contract_id=”orders”, contract_version=”1.0.0”, profile=”data_engineering”, checks=checks, data_reference={“s3”: “s3://bucket/data.parquet”}, callback_url=”https://registry/api/v1/jobs/job-123/callback”,

)

# split.wasm_job contains null_check # split.container_jobs contains drift_check

Parameters:

  • default_wasm_timeout (int)

  • default_container_timeout (int)

__init__(default_wasm_timeout=600, default_container_timeout=600)[source]¶

Initialize the job splitter.

Parameters:

  • default_wasm_timeout (int) – Default timeout for WASM worker (seconds)

  • default_container_timeout (int) – Default timeout for container checks (seconds)

split(job_id, contract_id, contract_version, profile, checks, data_reference, callback_url=None, metadata=None)[source]¶

Split a validation job into WASM and container sub-jobs.

Parameters:

  • job_id (str) – Parent job ID

  • contract_id (str) – Contract being validated

  • contract_version (str) – Version of the contract

  • profile (str) – Validation profile

  • checks (list[CheckSpec]) – List of checks to execute

  • data_reference (dict[str, Any]) – Reference to data (S3 URL, etc.)

  • callback_url (str | None) – URL to POST results back to

  • metadata (dict[str, Any] | None) – Additional job metadata

Returns:

SplitJob with separated WASM and container jobs

Return type:

SplitJob

split_from_contract(job_id, contract, profile, data_reference, callback_url=None, metadata=None)[source]¶

Split a job using a Contract object directly.

Extracts checks from the contract and converts them to CheckSpecs.

Parameters:

  • job_id (str) – Parent job ID

  • contract (Any) – Contract object

  • profile (str) – Validation profile

  • data_reference (dict[str, Any]) – Reference to data

  • callback_url (str | None) – URL to POST results back to

  • metadata (dict[str, Any] | None) – Additional job metadata

Returns:

SplitJob with separated WASM and container jobs

Return type:

SplitJob

class griot_core.orchestration.ResultAggregator(split_job)[source]¶

Bases: object

Aggregates results from parallel WASM and container executions.

Collects results as they complete and combines them into a final AggregatedResult when all jobs have finished.

Example

aggregator = ResultAggregator(split_job)

# As results come in from callbacks aggregator.add_wasm_result(wasm_callback_data) aggregator.add_container_result(container_1_callback_data) aggregator.add_container_result(container_2_callback_data)

# When all complete if aggregator.is_complete:

result = aggregator.aggregate()

Parameters:

split_job (SplitJob)

__init__(split_job)[source]¶

Initialize the aggregator.

Parameters:

split_job (SplitJob) – The split job containing expected WASM and container jobs

add_container_result(result)[source]¶

Add result from container check.

Parameters:

result (dict[str, Any]) – Callback data from container check

Return type:

None

add_result(result)[source]¶

Add a result, automatically routing to WASM or container handler.

Parameters:

result (dict[str, Any]) – Callback data

Return type:

None

add_wasm_result(result)[source]¶

Add result from WASM worker.

Parameters:

result (dict[str, Any]) – Callback data from WASM worker

Return type:

None

aggregate()[source]¶

Aggregate all results into a final result.

Returns:

AggregatedResult combining all check results

Return type:

AggregatedResult

get_progress()[source]¶

Get current aggregation progress.

Returns:

Progress information

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

property is_complete: bool¶

Whether all expected results have been received.

property pending_jobs: list[str]¶

List of job IDs that haven’t completed yet.

start()[source]¶

Mark the aggregation as started.

Return type:

None

class griot_core.orchestration.ValidationOrchestrator(dispatcher_config=None, dispatcher=None, splitter=None, callback_base_url=None, **dispatcher_kwargs)[source]¶

Bases: object

Orchestrates validation job execution across compute backends.

The orchestrator: 1. Splits jobs into WASM and container checks using JobSplitter 2. Dispatches all jobs in parallel to the compute backend 3. Collects results via callbacks (async) or polling 4. Aggregates results into a single AggregatedResult

This approach avoids Docker-in-Docker by: - Running WASM checks in a griot-core worker container with embedded WASM runtime - Running container checks as native K8s pods spawned by the orchestrator

Example

# Create orchestrator config = DispatcherConfig(

backend=ComputeBackend.KUBERNETES, wasm_worker_image=”griot/wasm-worker:v1.0”,

) orchestrator = ValidationOrchestrator(

dispatcher_config=config, namespace=”griot”,

)

# Execute validation result = await orchestrator.validate(

contract=contract, profile=”data_engineering”, data_reference={“s3”: “s3://bucket/data.parquet”},

)

print(f”Valid: {result.is_valid}”) print(f”Passed: {result.passed_checks}/{result.total_checks}”)

Parameters:

  • dispatcher_config (DispatcherConfig | None)

  • dispatcher (ComputeDispatcher | None)

  • splitter (JobSplitter | None)

  • callback_base_url (str | None)

  • dispatcher_kwargs (Any)

__init__(dispatcher_config=None, dispatcher=None, splitter=None, callback_base_url=None, **dispatcher_kwargs)[source]¶

Initialize the orchestrator.

Parameters:

  • dispatcher_config (DispatcherConfig | None) – Configuration for the dispatcher

  • dispatcher (ComputeDispatcher | None) – Pre-configured dispatcher (alternative to config)

  • splitter (JobSplitter | None) – Pre-configured job splitter

  • callback_base_url (str | None) – Base URL for job callbacks

  • **dispatcher_kwargs (Any) – Additional kwargs passed to dispatcher factory

property dispatcher: ComputeDispatcher¶

Get or create the compute dispatcher.

get_aggregator(job_id)[source]¶

Get the aggregator for a job.

Parameters:

job_id (str) – Parent job ID

Returns:

ResultAggregator or None if not found

Return type:

ResultAggregator | None

async health_check()[source]¶

Check orchestrator health.

Returns:

Health status information

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

receive_callback(job_id, result)[source]¶

Receive a callback result from a completed job.

Called by the callback endpoint to deliver results to the appropriate aggregator.

Parameters:

  • job_id (str) – Job ID from the callback

  • result (dict[str, Any]) – Result data from the worker

Returns:

True if callback was processed

Return type:

bool

async validate(contract, profile, data_reference, callback_url=None, metadata=None, wait_for_completion=False, timeout_seconds=1800)[source]¶

Execute validation for a contract.

This is the main entry point for validation. It: 1. Generates a job ID 2. Splits the job into WASM and container checks 3. Dispatches all jobs in parallel 4. Optionally waits for completion

Parameters:

  • contract (Any) – Contract to validate

  • profile (str) – Validation profile

  • data_reference (dict[str, Any]) – Reference to data (S3 URL, etc.) - NOT the data itself

  • callback_url (str | None) – Optional override for callback URL

  • metadata (dict[str, Any] | None) – Additional job metadata

  • wait_for_completion (bool) – If True, wait for all results synchronously

  • timeout_seconds (int) – Max wait time when waiting for completion

Returns:

AggregatedResult Otherwise: job_id (results come via callbacks)

Return type:

If wait_for_completion

async validate_checks(job_id, contract_id, contract_version, profile, checks, data_reference, callback_url=None, metadata=None)[source]¶

Execute validation with explicit checks (no contract object).

Lower-level API that accepts CheckSpecs directly instead of extracting them from a contract.

Parameters:

  • job_id (str) – Job ID to use

  • contract_id (str) – Contract identifier

  • contract_version (str) – Contract version

  • profile (str) – Validation profile

  • checks (list[CheckSpec]) – List of checks to execute

  • data_reference (dict[str, Any]) – Reference to data

  • callback_url (str | None) – URL to POST results back to

  • metadata (dict[str, Any] | None) – Additional job metadata

Returns:

Tuple of (SplitJob, list of DispatchResults)

Return type:

tuple[SplitJob, list[DispatchResult]]

class griot_core.orchestration.ComputeBackend(*values)[source]¶

Bases: str, Enum

Supported compute backends.

AWS_LAMBDA = 'aws_lambda'¶
KUBERNETES = 'kubernetes'¶
CLOUD_RUN = 'cloud_run'¶
LOCAL = 'local'¶
class griot_core.orchestration.ComputeDispatcher(config)[source]¶

Bases: ABC

Abstract base class for compute dispatchers.

This new interface separates WASM worker dispatch from container dispatch:

  • dispatch_wasm_worker(): Dispatches all WASM checks to a single griot-core worker container. The worker runs all WASM checks sequentially using the embedded WASM runtime.

  • dispatch_container(): Dispatches a single container check as a native pod/function. This avoids Docker-in-Docker by running containers directly on the compute backend.

Example usage:

dispatcher = KubernetesDispatcher(config)

# Split job has WASM and container checks if split_job.has_wasm_checks:

result = await dispatcher.dispatch_wasm_worker(split_job.wasm_job)

for container_job in split_job.container_jobs:

result = await dispatcher.dispatch_container(container_job)

Parameters:

config (DispatcherConfig)

__init__(config)[source]¶

Initialize dispatcher with configuration.

Parameters:

config (DispatcherConfig) – Dispatcher configuration

abstract property backend: ComputeBackend¶

Return the compute backend type.

abstractmethod async cancel(invocation_id)[source]¶

Cancel a running job.

Parameters:

invocation_id (str) – The invocation ID to cancel

Returns:

True if cancellation was successful

Return type:

bool

abstractmethod async check_status(invocation_id)[source]¶

Check the status of a dispatched job.

Parameters:

invocation_id (str) – The invocation ID from DispatchResult

Returns:

Backend-specific status information

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

abstractmethod async dispatch_container(spec)[source]¶

Dispatch a single container check as a native pod/function.

Creates a native K8s pod, Lambda function, or Cloud Run job that runs the container check directly. This avoids Docker-in-Docker by letting the orchestrator spawn the container.

Parameters:

spec (ContainerJobSpec) – Container job specification for a single check

Returns:

DispatchResult indicating success or failure

Return type:

DispatchResult

abstractmethod async dispatch_wasm_worker(spec)[source]¶

Dispatch WASM checks to a griot-core worker container.

Creates a single worker container that runs all WASM checks sequentially. The worker uses the embedded WASM runtime (no Docker/Podman needed inside the container).

Parameters:

spec (WasmJobSpec) – WASM job specification with all WASM checks

Returns:

DispatchResult indicating success or failure

Return type:

DispatchResult

async health_check()[source]¶

Check if the compute backend is healthy.

Returns:

True if the backend is available

Return type:

bool

griot_core.orchestration.create_dispatcher(config, **kwargs)[source]¶

Create a compute dispatcher based on configuration.

Factory function that instantiates the appropriate dispatcher for the configured backend.

Parameters:

  • config (DispatcherConfig) – Dispatcher configuration specifying the backend

  • **kwargs (Any) – Additional backend-specific arguments

Returns:

Configured ComputeDispatcher instance

Raises:

ValueError – If the backend is not supported

Return type:

ComputeDispatcher

Example

# Kubernetes dispatcher config = DispatcherConfig(

backend=ComputeBackend.KUBERNETES, wasm_worker_image=”griot/wasm-worker:v1.0”,

) dispatcher = create_dispatcher(

config, namespace=”griot”,

)

# Lambda dispatcher config = DispatcherConfig(

backend=ComputeBackend.AWS_LAMBDA,

) dispatcher = create_dispatcher(

config, wasm_function_name=”griot-wasm-worker”,

)

griot_core.orchestration.create_dispatcher_from_dict(config_dict, **kwargs)[source]¶

Create a dispatcher from a configuration dictionary.

Convenience function that creates a DispatcherConfig from a dict and then creates the appropriate dispatcher.

Parameters:

  • config_dict (dict[str, Any]) – Dictionary with configuration values

  • **kwargs (Any) – Additional backend-specific arguments

Returns:

Configured ComputeDispatcher instance

Return type:

ComputeDispatcher

Example

config = {

“backend”: “kubernetes”, “wasm_worker_image”: “griot/wasm-worker:v1.0”, “timeout_seconds”: 600,

} dispatcher = create_dispatcher_from_dict(

config, namespace=”griot”,

)

class griot_core.orchestration.KubernetesDispatcher(config, namespace='default', service_account=None)[source]¶

Bases: ComputeDispatcher

Kubernetes dispatcher for orchestrated validation jobs.

Creates native K8s Jobs for both WASM workers and container checks. This avoids Docker-in-Docker by having the orchestrator spawn containers directly on Kubernetes.

WASM checks:

All WASM checks are bundled into a single griot-core worker pod that runs them sequentially using the embedded WASM runtime.

Container checks:

Each container check runs as a separate K8s Job with the check’s container image as the pod spec.

Example

config = DispatcherConfig(

backend=ComputeBackend.KUBERNETES, wasm_worker_image=”griot/wasm-worker:v1.0”,

) dispatcher = KubernetesDispatcher(

config, namespace=”griot”,

)

# Dispatch WASM checks result = await dispatcher.dispatch_wasm_worker(wasm_spec)

# Dispatch container check result = await dispatcher.dispatch_container(container_spec)

Parameters:

  • config (DispatcherConfig)

  • namespace (str)

  • service_account (str | None)

__init__(config, namespace='default', service_account=None)[source]¶

Initialize Kubernetes dispatcher.

Parameters:

  • config (DispatcherConfig) – Dispatcher configuration

  • namespace (str) – Kubernetes namespace to create jobs in

  • service_account (str | None) – Optional service account for jobs

property backend: ComputeBackend¶

Return Kubernetes backend type.

Return type:

bool

Returns:

dict[str, Any]

Dispatch a container check as a native K8s Job.

Creates a K8s Job running the check’s container image directly. This avoids Docker-in-Docker.

spec (ContainerJobSpec) – Container job specification

Returns:

DispatchResult with job creation status

Return type:

DispatchResult

Creates a K8s Job running the griot-core WASM worker image. The worker executes all WASM checks sequentially.

Parameters:

spec (WasmJobSpec) – WASM job specification

Returns:

DispatchResult with job creation status

Return type:

DispatchResult

Return type:

bool

class griot_core.orchestration.LambdaDispatcher(config, wasm_function_name='griot-wasm-worker', container_function_prefix='griot-check-')[source]¶

Bases: ComputeDispatcher

AWS Lambda dispatcher for orchestrated validation jobs.

Uses Lambda’s async invocation for fire-and-forget execution. Results are posted back to the callback URL.

WASM checks:

Invokes a Lambda function running griot-core WASM worker. All WASM checks are executed sequentially in one invocation.

Container checks:

Invokes Lambda functions deployed with container images. Each check is a separate Lambda invocation.

Example

config = DispatcherConfig(

backend=ComputeBackend.AWS_LAMBDA, environment={“GRIOT_REGISTRY_URL”: “https://…”},

) dispatcher = LambdaDispatcher(

config, wasm_function_name=”griot-wasm-worker”,

)

Parameters:

  • config (DispatcherConfig)

  • wasm_function_name (str)

  • container_function_prefix (str)

__init__(config, wasm_function_name='griot-wasm-worker', container_function_prefix='griot-check-')[source]¶

Initialize Lambda dispatcher.

Parameters:

  • config (DispatcherConfig) – Dispatcher configuration

  • wasm_function_name (str) – Lambda function name for WASM worker

  • container_function_prefix (str) – Prefix for container check function names

property backend: ComputeBackend¶

Return AWS Lambda backend type.

async cancel(invocation_id)[source]¶

Cancel a Lambda invocation.

Lambda async invocations cannot be cancelled once started.

Parameters:

invocation_id (str) – The request ID to cancel

Returns:

False - Lambda async invocations cannot be cancelled

Return type:

bool

async check_status(invocation_id)[source]¶

Check Lambda invocation status.

Lambda async invocations don’t provide status tracking. Status comes from the worker callback.

Parameters:

invocation_id (str) – The request ID from dispatch

Returns:

Status information (limited for async Lambda)

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

async dispatch_container(spec)[source]¶

Dispatch container check to Lambda function.

The Lambda function should be deployed with the check’s container image using Lambda’s container image support.

Parameters:

spec (ContainerJobSpec) – Container job specification

Returns:

DispatchResult with invocation status

Return type:

DispatchResult

async dispatch_wasm_worker(spec)[source]¶

Dispatch WASM checks to Lambda function.

Uses async invocation so the registry doesn’t wait for completion. The worker POSTs results to callback_url.

Parameters:

spec (WasmJobSpec) – WASM job specification

Returns:

DispatchResult with invocation status

Return type:

DispatchResult

async health_check()[source]¶

Check if Lambda function is available.

Returns:

True if WASM worker function exists and is active

Return type:

bool

class griot_core.orchestration.CloudRunDispatcher(config, project_id=None, region='us-central1', wasm_service_url=None, use_auth=True)[source]¶

Bases: ComputeDispatcher

Google Cloud Run dispatcher for orchestrated validation jobs.

Invokes Cloud Run services via HTTP. Services process asynchronously and POST results back to callback URLs.

WASM checks:

Invokes a Cloud Run service running griot-core WASM worker. All WASM checks are executed in one service invocation.

Container checks:

Invokes Cloud Run Jobs for container checks. Each check runs as a separate Cloud Run Job.

Example

config = DispatcherConfig(

backend=ComputeBackend.CLOUD_RUN,

) dispatcher = CloudRunDispatcher(

config, project_id=”my-project”, region=”us-central1”, wasm_service_url=”https://griot-wasm-worker-xxx.run.app”,

)

Parameters:

  • config (DispatcherConfig)

  • project_id (str | None)

  • region (str)

  • wasm_service_url (str | None)

  • use_auth (bool)

Initialize Cloud Run dispatcher.

Parameters:

  • config (DispatcherConfig) – Dispatcher configuration

  • project_id (str | None) – GCP project ID

  • region (str) – Cloud Run region

  • wasm_service_url (str | None) – URL of the WASM worker Cloud Run service

  • use_auth (bool) – Whether to use GCP authentication

Return Cloud Run backend type.

dict[str, Any]

spec (ContainerJobSpec) – Container job specification

Returns:

DispatchResult with invocation status

Return type:

DispatchResult

Sends HTTP POST to the WASM worker service. The service processes asynchronously and POSTs results to callback URL.

Parameters:

DispatchResult with invocation status

Return type:

DispatchResult

bool

class griot_core.orchestration.LocalDispatcher(config, max_workers=4, callback_timeout=30.0)[source]¶

Bases: ComputeDispatcher

Local dispatcher for development and testing.

Executes validation jobs in-process using griot-core. Results are posted to the callback URL after completion.

WARNING: This is for development only. In production, use Kubernetes, Lambda, or Cloud Run.

Example

config = DispatcherConfig(

backend=ComputeBackend.LOCAL, timeout_seconds=60,

) dispatcher = LocalDispatcher(config) result = await dispatcher.dispatch_wasm_worker(wasm_spec)

Parameters:

  • config (DispatcherConfig)

  • max_workers (int)

  • callback_timeout (float)

__init__(config, max_workers=4, callback_timeout=30.0)[source]¶

Initialize local dispatcher.

Parameters:

  • config (DispatcherConfig) – Dispatcher configuration

  • max_workers (int) – Max concurrent job threads

  • callback_timeout (float) – Timeout for callback POST requests

True if cancellation was successful

bool

Parameters:

invocation_id (str) – The local invocation ID

Returns:

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

For local testing, runs container checks using the container runtime if available (podman/docker).

spec (ContainerJobSpec) – Container job specification

Returns:

DispatchResult

Runs WASM checks in a background task using griot-core’s WASM runtime.

spec (WasmJobSpec) – WASM job specification

Return type:

DispatchResult

bool

None

Type definitions for orchestration.

Defines the data structures used for job splitting, dispatching, and result aggregation in the orchestration layer.

class griot_core.orchestration.types.ContainerJobSpec(job_id, parent_job_id, contract_id, contract_version, check, data_reference, callback_url=None, image=None, timeout_seconds=600, resource_limits=<factory>, metadata=<factory>)[source]¶

Specification for a single container check job.

Each container check runs as a separate native K8s pod to avoid Docker-in-Docker.

Parameters:

  • job_id (str)

  • parent_job_id (str)

  • contract_id (str)

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

Job splitter for separating WASM and container checks.

The JobSplitter analyzes a validation job’s checks and separates them by runtime type: - WASM checks -> batched into a single WasmJobSpec - Container checks -> each becomes a separate ContainerJobSpec

This enables parallel execution without Docker-in-Docker.

class griot_core.orchestration.splitter.JobSplitter(default_wasm_timeout=600, default_container_timeout=600)[source]¶

Splits validation jobs into WASM and container sub-jobs.

The splitter categorizes checks by their runtime type and creates appropriate job specifications for each:

  • WASM checks are batched into a single WasmJobSpec that will run in a griot-core worker container using the embedded WASM runtime.

  • Container checks each become a separate ContainerJobSpec that will run as a native K8s pod (avoiding Docker-in-Docker).

Example

splitter = JobSplitter()

# Contract has mixed checks checks = [

CheckSpec(name=”null_check”, executor_uri=”registry://null-check@1.0”, runtime=CheckRuntime.WASM), CheckSpec(name=”drift_check”, executor_uri=”oci://ghcr.io/griot/drift:1.0”, runtime=CheckRuntime.CONTAINER),

]

split = splitter.split(

job_id=”job-123”, contract_id=”orders”, contract_version=”1.0.0”, profile=”data_engineering”, checks=checks, data_reference={“s3”: “s3://bucket/data.parquet”}, callback_url=”https://registry/api/v1/jobs/job-123/callback”,

)

# split.wasm_job contains null_check # split.container_jobs contains drift_check

Parameters:

  • default_wasm_timeout (int)

  • default_container_timeout (int)

__init__(default_wasm_timeout=600, default_container_timeout=600)[source]¶

Initialize the job splitter.

Parameters:

  • default_wasm_timeout (int) – Default timeout for WASM worker (seconds)

  • default_container_timeout (int) – Default timeout for container checks (seconds)

split(job_id, contract_id, contract_version, profile, checks, data_reference, callback_url=None, metadata=None)[source]¶

Split a validation job into WASM and container sub-jobs.

Parameters:

  • job_id (str) – Parent job ID

  • contract_id (str) – Contract being validated

  • contract_version (str) – Version of the contract

  • profile (str) – Validation profile

  • checks (list[CheckSpec]) – List of checks to execute

  • data_reference (dict[str, Any]) – Reference to data (S3 URL, etc.)

  • callback_url (str | None) – URL to POST results back to

  • metadata (dict[str, Any] | None) – Additional job metadata

Returns:

SplitJob with separated WASM and container jobs

Return type:

SplitJob

split_from_contract(job_id, contract, profile, data_reference, callback_url=None, metadata=None)[source]¶

Split a job using a Contract object directly.

Extracts checks from the contract and converts them to CheckSpecs.

Parameters:

  • job_id (str) – Parent job ID

  • contract (Any) – Contract object

  • profile (str) – Validation profile

  • data_reference (dict[str, Any]) – Reference to data

  • callback_url (str | None) – URL to POST results back to

  • metadata (dict[str, Any] | None) – Additional job metadata

Returns:

SplitJob with separated WASM and container jobs

Return type:

SplitJob

Result aggregator for combining parallel execution results.

The ResultAggregator collects results from WASM worker and container checks executed in parallel, combining them into a single AggregatedResult.

class griot_core.orchestration.aggregator.ResultAggregator(split_job)[source]¶

Aggregates results from parallel WASM and container executions.

Collects results as they complete and combines them into a final AggregatedResult when all jobs have finished.

Example

aggregator = ResultAggregator(split_job)

# As results come in from callbacks aggregator.add_wasm_result(wasm_callback_data) aggregator.add_container_result(container_1_callback_data) aggregator.add_container_result(container_2_callback_data)

# When all complete if aggregator.is_complete:

result = aggregator.aggregate()

Parameters:

split_job (SplitJob)

__init__(split_job)[source]¶

Initialize the aggregator.

Parameters:

split_job (SplitJob) – The split job containing expected WASM and container jobs

property is_complete: bool¶

Whether all expected results have been received.

property pending_jobs: list[str]¶

List of job IDs that haven’t completed yet.

start()[source]¶

Mark the aggregation as started.

Return type:

None

add_wasm_result(result)[source]¶

Add result from WASM worker.

Parameters:

result (dict[str, Any]) – Callback data from WASM worker

Return type:

None

add_container_result(result)[source]¶

Add result from container check.

Parameters:

result (dict[str, Any]) – Callback data from container check

Return type:

None

add_result(result)[source]¶

Add a result, automatically routing to WASM or container handler.

Parameters:

result (dict[str, Any]) – Callback data

Return type:

None

aggregate()[source]¶

Aggregate all results into a final result.

Returns:

AggregatedResult combining all check results

Return type:

AggregatedResult

get_progress()[source]¶

Get current aggregation progress.

Returns:

Progress information

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

Validation orchestrator - main entry point for orchestrated validation.

The ValidationOrchestrator coordinates the execution of validation jobs by splitting them into WASM and container checks, dispatching them in parallel, and aggregating the results.

class griot_core.orchestration.orchestrator.ValidationOrchestrator(dispatcher_config=None, dispatcher=None, splitter=None, callback_base_url=None, **dispatcher_kwargs)[source]¶

Orchestrates validation job execution across compute backends.

The orchestrator: 1. Splits jobs into WASM and container checks using JobSplitter 2. Dispatches all jobs in parallel to the compute backend 3. Collects results via callbacks (async) or polling 4. Aggregates results into a single AggregatedResult

This approach avoids Docker-in-Docker by: - Running WASM checks in a griot-core worker container with embedded WASM runtime - Running container checks as native K8s pods spawned by the orchestrator

Example

# Create orchestrator config = DispatcherConfig(

backend=ComputeBackend.KUBERNETES, wasm_worker_image=”griot/wasm-worker:v1.0”,

) orchestrator = ValidationOrchestrator(

dispatcher_config=config, namespace=”griot”,

)

# Execute validation result = await orchestrator.validate(

contract=contract, profile=”data_engineering”, data_reference={“s3”: “s3://bucket/data.parquet”},

)

print(f”Valid: {result.is_valid}”) print(f”Passed: {result.passed_checks}/{result.total_checks}”)

Parameters:

  • dispatcher_config (DispatcherConfig | None)

  • dispatcher (ComputeDispatcher | None)

  • splitter (JobSplitter | None)

  • callback_base_url (str | None)

  • dispatcher_kwargs (Any)

__init__(dispatcher_config=None, dispatcher=None, splitter=None, callback_base_url=None, **dispatcher_kwargs)[source]¶

Initialize the orchestrator.

Parameters:

  • dispatcher_config (DispatcherConfig | None) – Configuration for the dispatcher

  • dispatcher (ComputeDispatcher | None) – Pre-configured dispatcher (alternative to config)

  • splitter (JobSplitter | None) – Pre-configured job splitter

  • callback_base_url (str | None) – Base URL for job callbacks

  • **dispatcher_kwargs (Any) – Additional kwargs passed to dispatcher factory

property dispatcher: ComputeDispatcher¶

Get or create the compute dispatcher.

async validate(contract, profile, data_reference, callback_url=None, metadata=None, wait_for_completion=False, timeout_seconds=1800)[source]¶

Execute validation for a contract.

This is the main entry point for validation. It: 1. Generates a job ID 2. Splits the job into WASM and container checks 3. Dispatches all jobs in parallel 4. Optionally waits for completion

Parameters:

  • contract (Any) – Contract to validate

  • profile (str) – Validation profile

  • data_reference (dict[str, Any]) – Reference to data (S3 URL, etc.) - NOT the data itself

  • callback_url (str | None) – Optional override for callback URL

  • metadata (dict[str, Any] | None) – Additional job metadata

  • wait_for_completion (bool) – If True, wait for all results synchronously

  • timeout_seconds (int) – Max wait time when waiting for completion

Returns:

AggregatedResult Otherwise: job_id (results come via callbacks)

Return type:

If wait_for_completion

async validate_checks(job_id, contract_id, contract_version, profile, checks, data_reference, callback_url=None, metadata=None)[source]¶

Execute validation with explicit checks (no contract object).

Lower-level API that accepts CheckSpecs directly instead of extracting them from a contract.

Parameters:

  • job_id (str) – Job ID to use

  • contract_id (str) – Contract identifier

  • contract_version (str) – Contract version

  • profile (str) – Validation profile

  • checks (list[CheckSpec]) – List of checks to execute

  • data_reference (dict[str, Any]) – Reference to data

  • callback_url (str | None) – URL to POST results back to

  • metadata (dict[str, Any] | None) – Additional job metadata

Returns:

Tuple of (SplitJob, list of DispatchResults)

Return type:

tuple[SplitJob, list[DispatchResult]]

receive_callback(job_id, result)[source]¶

Receive a callback result from a completed job.

Called by the callback endpoint to deliver results to the appropriate aggregator.

Parameters:

  • job_id (str) – Job ID from the callback

  • result (dict[str, Any]) – Result data from the worker

Returns:

True if callback was processed

Return type:

bool

get_aggregator(job_id)[source]¶

Get the aggregator for a job.

Parameters:

job_id (str) – Parent job ID

Returns:

ResultAggregator or None if not found

Return type:

ResultAggregator | None

async health_check()[source]¶

Check orchestrator health.

Returns:

Health status information

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

Compute dispatchers for orchestrated validation jobs.

This module provides the dispatcher implementations for different compute backends (Kubernetes, Lambda, Cloud Run, Local).

The key difference from the old griot-registry dispatchers:

Old approach (Docker-in-Docker):

Registry -> K8s Pod (worker) -> Docker/Podman (container check)

New approach (Native pods):
Registry -> Orchestrator -> K8s Pod (WASM worker)

-> K8s Pod (container check A) -> K8s Pod (container check B)

class griot_core.orchestration.dispatcher.ComputeBackend(*values)[source]¶

Supported compute backends.

class griot_core.orchestration.dispatcher.ComputeDispatcher(config)[source]¶

Abstract base class for compute dispatchers.

This new interface separates WASM worker dispatch from container dispatch:

  • dispatch_wasm_worker(): Dispatches all WASM checks to a single griot-core worker container. The worker runs all WASM checks sequentially using the embedded WASM runtime.

  • dispatch_container(): Dispatches a single container check as a native pod/function. This avoids Docker-in-Docker by running containers directly on the compute backend.

Example usage:

dispatcher = KubernetesDispatcher(config)

# Split job has WASM and container checks if split_job.has_wasm_checks:

result = await dispatcher.dispatch_wasm_worker(split_job.wasm_job)

for container_job in split_job.container_jobs:

result = await dispatcher.dispatch_container(container_job)

Parameters:

config (DispatcherConfig)

__init__(config)[source]¶

Initialize dispatcher with configuration.

Parameters:

config (DispatcherConfig) – Dispatcher configuration

abstract property backend: ComputeBackend¶

Return the compute backend type.

abstractmethod async cancel(invocation_id)[source]¶

Cancel a running job.

Parameters:

invocation_id (str) – The invocation ID to cancel

Returns:

True if cancellation was successful

Return type:

bool

abstractmethod async check_status(invocation_id)[source]¶

Check the status of a dispatched job.

Parameters:

invocation_id (str) – The invocation ID from DispatchResult

Returns:

Backend-specific status information

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

abstractmethod async dispatch_container(spec)[source]¶

Dispatch a single container check as a native pod/function.

Creates a native K8s pod, Lambda function, or Cloud Run job that runs the container check directly. This avoids Docker-in-Docker by letting the orchestrator spawn the container.

Parameters:

spec (ContainerJobSpec) – Container job specification for a single check

Returns:

DispatchResult indicating success or failure

Return type:

DispatchResult

abstractmethod async dispatch_wasm_worker(spec)[source]¶

Dispatch WASM checks to a griot-core worker container.

Creates a single worker container that runs all WASM checks sequentially. The worker uses the embedded WASM runtime (no Docker/Podman needed inside the container).

Parameters:

spec (WasmJobSpec) – WASM job specification with all WASM checks

Returns:

DispatchResult indicating success or failure

Return type:

DispatchResult

async health_check()[source]¶

Check if the compute backend is healthy.

Returns:

True if the backend is available

Return type:

bool

griot_core.orchestration.dispatcher.create_dispatcher(config, **kwargs)[source]¶

Create a compute dispatcher based on configuration.

Factory function that instantiates the appropriate dispatcher for the configured backend.

Parameters:

  • config (DispatcherConfig) – Dispatcher configuration specifying the backend

  • **kwargs (Any) – Additional backend-specific arguments

Returns:

Configured ComputeDispatcher instance

Raises:

ValueError – If the backend is not supported

Return type:

ComputeDispatcher

Example

# Kubernetes dispatcher config = DispatcherConfig(

backend=ComputeBackend.KUBERNETES, wasm_worker_image=”griot/wasm-worker:v1.0”,

) dispatcher = create_dispatcher(

config, namespace=”griot”,

)

# Lambda dispatcher config = DispatcherConfig(

backend=ComputeBackend.AWS_LAMBDA,

) dispatcher = create_dispatcher(

config, wasm_function_name=”griot-wasm-worker”,

)

griot_core.orchestration.dispatcher.create_dispatcher_from_dict(config_dict, **kwargs)[source]¶

Create a dispatcher from a configuration dictionary.

Convenience function that creates a DispatcherConfig from a dict and then creates the appropriate dispatcher.

Parameters:

  • config_dict (dict[str, Any]) – Dictionary with configuration values

  • **kwargs (Any) – Additional backend-specific arguments

Returns:

Configured ComputeDispatcher instance

Return type:

ComputeDispatcher

Example

config = {

“backend”: “kubernetes”, “wasm_worker_image”: “griot/wasm-worker:v1.0”, “timeout_seconds”: 600,

} dispatcher = create_dispatcher_from_dict(

config, namespace=”griot”,

)

class griot_core.orchestration.dispatcher.KubernetesDispatcher(config, namespace='default', service_account=None)[source]¶

Kubernetes dispatcher for orchestrated validation jobs.

Creates native K8s Jobs for both WASM workers and container checks. This avoids Docker-in-Docker by having the orchestrator spawn containers directly on Kubernetes.

WASM checks:

All WASM checks are bundled into a single griot-core worker pod that runs them sequentially using the embedded WASM runtime.

Container checks:

Each container check runs as a separate K8s Job with the check’s container image as the pod spec.

Example

config = DispatcherConfig(

backend=ComputeBackend.KUBERNETES, wasm_worker_image=”griot/wasm-worker:v1.0”,

) dispatcher = KubernetesDispatcher(

config, namespace=”griot”,

)

# Dispatch WASM checks result = await dispatcher.dispatch_wasm_worker(wasm_spec)

# Dispatch container check result = await dispatcher.dispatch_container(container_spec)

Parameters:

  • config (DispatcherConfig)

  • namespace (str)

  • service_account (str | None)

__init__(config, namespace='default', service_account=None)[source]¶

Initialize Kubernetes dispatcher.

Parameters:

  • config (DispatcherConfig) – Dispatcher configuration

  • namespace (str) – Kubernetes namespace to create jobs in

  • service_account (str | None) – Optional service account for jobs

property backend: ComputeBackend¶

Return Kubernetes backend type.

async cancel(invocation_id)[source]¶

Cancel (delete) a Kubernetes Job.

Parameters:

invocation_id (str) – The namespace/job-name to cancel

Returns:

True if deletion was successful

Return type:

bool

async check_status(invocation_id)[source]¶

Check Kubernetes Job status.

Parameters:

invocation_id (str) – The namespace/job-name from dispatch

Returns:

Job status information

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

async dispatch_container(spec)[source]¶

Dispatch a container check as a native K8s Job.

Creates a K8s Job running the check’s container image directly. This avoids Docker-in-Docker.

Parameters:

spec (ContainerJobSpec) – Container job specification

Returns:

DispatchResult with job creation status

Return type:

DispatchResult

async dispatch_wasm_worker(spec)[source]¶

Dispatch WASM checks to a griot-core worker pod.

Creates a K8s Job running the griot-core WASM worker image. The worker executes all WASM checks sequentially.

Parameters:

spec (WasmJobSpec) – WASM job specification

Returns:

DispatchResult with job creation status

Return type:

DispatchResult

async health_check()[source]¶

Check if Kubernetes API is accessible.

Returns:

True if K8s API is reachable

Return type:

bool

class griot_core.orchestration.dispatcher.LambdaDispatcher(config, wasm_function_name='griot-wasm-worker', container_function_prefix='griot-check-')[source]¶

AWS Lambda dispatcher for orchestrated validation jobs.

Uses Lambda’s async invocation for fire-and-forget execution. Results are posted back to the callback URL.

WASM checks:

Invokes a Lambda function running griot-core WASM worker. All WASM checks are executed sequentially in one invocation.

Container checks:

Invokes Lambda functions deployed with container images. Each check is a separate Lambda invocation.

Example

config = DispatcherConfig(

backend=ComputeBackend.AWS_LAMBDA, environment={“GRIOT_REGISTRY_URL”: “https://…”},

) dispatcher = LambdaDispatcher(

config, wasm_function_name=”griot-wasm-worker”,

)

Parameters:

  • config (DispatcherConfig)

  • wasm_function_name (str)

  • container_function_prefix (str)

__init__(config, wasm_function_name='griot-wasm-worker', container_function_prefix='griot-check-')[source]¶

Initialize Lambda dispatcher.

Parameters:

  • config (DispatcherConfig) – Dispatcher configuration

  • wasm_function_name (str) – Lambda function name for WASM worker

  • container_function_prefix (str) – Prefix for container check function names

property backend: ComputeBackend¶

Return AWS Lambda backend type.

async cancel(invocation_id)[source]¶

Cancel a Lambda invocation.

Lambda async invocations cannot be cancelled once started.

Parameters:

invocation_id (str) – The request ID to cancel

Returns:

False - Lambda async invocations cannot be cancelled

Return type:

bool

async check_status(invocation_id)[source]¶

Check Lambda invocation status.

Lambda async invocations don’t provide status tracking. Status comes from the worker callback.

Parameters:

invocation_id (str) – The request ID from dispatch

Returns:

Status information (limited for async Lambda)

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

async dispatch_container(spec)[source]¶

Dispatch container check to Lambda function.

The Lambda function should be deployed with the check’s container image using Lambda’s container image support.

Parameters:

spec (ContainerJobSpec) – Container job specification

Returns:

DispatchResult with invocation status

Return type:

DispatchResult

async dispatch_wasm_worker(spec)[source]¶

Dispatch WASM checks to Lambda function.

Uses async invocation so the registry doesn’t wait for completion. The worker POSTs results to callback_url.

Parameters:

spec (WasmJobSpec) – WASM job specification

Returns:

DispatchResult with invocation status

Return type:

DispatchResult

async health_check()[source]¶

Check if Lambda function is available.

Returns:

True if WASM worker function exists and is active

Return type:

bool

class griot_core.orchestration.dispatcher.CloudRunDispatcher(config, project_id=None, region='us-central1', wasm_service_url=None, use_auth=True)[source]¶

Google Cloud Run dispatcher for orchestrated validation jobs.

Invokes Cloud Run services via HTTP. Services process asynchronously and POST results back to callback URLs.

WASM checks:

Invokes a Cloud Run service running griot-core WASM worker. All WASM checks are executed in one service invocation.

Container checks:

Invokes Cloud Run Jobs for container checks. Each check runs as a separate Cloud Run Job.

Example

config = DispatcherConfig(

backend=ComputeBackend.CLOUD_RUN,

) dispatcher = CloudRunDispatcher(

config, project_id=”my-project”, region=”us-central1”, wasm_service_url=”https://griot-wasm-worker-xxx.run.app”,

)

Parameters:

  • config (DispatcherConfig)

  • project_id (str | None)

  • region (str)

  • wasm_service_url (str | None)

  • use_auth (bool)

__init__(config, project_id=None, region='us-central1', wasm_service_url=None, use_auth=True)[source]¶

Initialize Cloud Run dispatcher.

Parameters:

  • config (DispatcherConfig) – Dispatcher configuration

  • project_id (str | None) – GCP project ID

  • region (str) – Cloud Run region

  • wasm_service_url (str | None) – URL of the WASM worker Cloud Run service

  • use_auth (bool) – Whether to use GCP authentication

property backend: ComputeBackend¶

Return Cloud Run backend type.

async cancel(invocation_id)[source]¶

Cancel a Cloud Run execution.

Parameters:

invocation_id (str) – The execution name to cancel

Returns:

True if cancellation was successful

Return type:

bool

async check_status(invocation_id)[source]¶

Check Cloud Run invocation status.

Parameters:

invocation_id (str) – The request ID or job name from dispatch

Returns:

Status information

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

async close()[source]¶

Close the HTTP client.

Return type:

None

async dispatch_container(spec)[source]¶

Dispatch container check as Cloud Run Job.

Creates a Cloud Run Job that runs the check’s container image. The job executes asynchronously.

Parameters:

spec (ContainerJobSpec) – Container job specification

Returns:

DispatchResult with invocation status

Return type:

DispatchResult

async dispatch_wasm_worker(spec)[source]¶

Dispatch WASM checks to Cloud Run service.

Sends HTTP POST to the WASM worker service. The service processes asynchronously and POSTs results to callback URL.

Parameters:

spec (WasmJobSpec) – WASM job specification

Returns:

DispatchResult with invocation status

Return type:

DispatchResult

async health_check()[source]¶

Check if Cloud Run service is healthy.

Returns:

True if WASM worker service responds

Return type:

bool

class griot_core.orchestration.dispatcher.LocalDispatcher(config, max_workers=4, callback_timeout=30.0)[source]¶

Local dispatcher for development and testing.

Executes validation jobs in-process using griot-core. Results are posted to the callback URL after completion.

WARNING: This is for development only. In production, use Kubernetes, Lambda, or Cloud Run.

Example

config = DispatcherConfig(

backend=ComputeBackend.LOCAL, timeout_seconds=60,

) dispatcher = LocalDispatcher(config) result = await dispatcher.dispatch_wasm_worker(wasm_spec)

Parameters:

  • config (DispatcherConfig)

  • max_workers (int)

  • callback_timeout (float)

__init__(config, max_workers=4, callback_timeout=30.0)[source]¶

Initialize local dispatcher.

Parameters:

  • config (DispatcherConfig) – Dispatcher configuration

  • max_workers (int) – Max concurrent job threads

  • callback_timeout (float) – Timeout for callback POST requests

property backend: ComputeBackend¶

Return local backend type.

async cancel(invocation_id)[source]¶

Cancel a local job.

Parameters:

invocation_id (str) – The invocation ID to cancel

Returns:

True if cancellation was successful

Return type:

bool

async check_status(invocation_id)[source]¶

Check local job status.

Parameters:

invocation_id (str) – The local invocation ID

Returns:

Job status information

Return type:

dict[str, Any]

async dispatch_container(spec)[source]¶

Dispatch container check for local execution.

For local testing, runs container checks using the container runtime if available (podman/docker).

Parameters:

spec (ContainerJobSpec) – Container job specification

Returns:

DispatchResult with local execution ID

Return type:

DispatchResult

async dispatch_wasm_worker(spec)[source]¶

Dispatch WASM checks for local execution.

Runs WASM checks in a background task using griot-core’s WASM runtime.

Parameters:

spec (WasmJobSpec) – WASM job specification

Returns:

DispatchResult with local execution ID

Return type:

DispatchResult

async health_check()[source]¶

Local dispatcher is always healthy.

Return type:

bool

async shutdown()[source]¶

Shutdown the dispatcher and cancel running jobs.

Return type:

None
